articles.title: Plan to scrap presidential elections puts Zimbabweans at loggerheads
articles.description: The ruling party has unveiled a draft law that strips voters of the chance to elect the president.

articles.title: Vic Falls allure spawns tourist rush
articles.description: Rutendo Nyeve-Bulawayo Bureau A RECORD number of local and international tourists are visiting the Victoria Falls as the waterfall reaches its peak, defying fears that numbers could drop due to... The post Vic Falls allure spawns tourist rush appeared first on herald .

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: 'Friend to All' - Zimbabwe Hails Foreign Policy Gains Since 2017
more.description: [263Chat] The government says its foreign policy of being a "friend to all and an enemy to none" has delivered major economic and diplomatic gains since 2017.

more.title: Ex-Health Registrar Wins Bail As He Appeals Abuse of Office Conviction
more.description: [263Chat] The High Court has granted US$500 bail to Collin Benyure, the former Registrar of the Medical and Dental Practitioners Council of Zimbabwe who was serving a 12-month jail term for criminal abuse of duty.

more.title: Chamisa claims ‘fake news’ as activists accuse him of selling out
more.description: Former Citizens Coalition for Change (CCC) leader Nelson Chamisa has hit back at what he calls a “propaganda campaign of fake news” after being accused by some opposition activists of “selling out the struggle for democracy”. The accusations stem from Chamisa’s perceived lack of vigour in opposing the controversial Constitutional Amendment Bill Number 3, which [...] The post Chamisa claims ‘fake news’ as activists accuse him of selling out appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: South Africa returns stolen human remains, sacred bird carving to Zimbabwe
more.description: AFP South Africa on Tuesday handed back to Zimbabwe ancestral human remains and a centuries-old stone carving of its sacred national emblem, the Zimbabwe bird, stolen more than 100 years ago during the colonial era. The restitution was part of a worldwide push for the repatriation of artefacts looted from African countries during colonisation. Eight [...] The post South Africa returns stolen human remains, sacred bird carving to Zimbabwe appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Troubled OK Zim closes Byo Lobengula St branch
more.description: Source: Troubled OK Zim closes Byo Lobengula St branch – herald Sikhulekelani Moyo, sikhu.moyo@chronicle.co.zw STRUGGLING retail giant OK Zimbabwe has closed its Lobengula branch in Bulawayo. This follows a plunge into “choppy waters” that has forced the 83-year-old retail chain to shut down several outlets across the country. Among the operations already affected are the Food [...] The post Troubled OK Zim closes Byo Lobengula St branch appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Bus imports now duty-free
more.description: Source: Bus imports now duty-free – herald Farirai Machivenyika Senior Reporter FINANCE, Economic Development and Investment Promotion Minister Professor Mthuli Ncube has suspended duty on public service bus imports backdated to January 1, this year. The initiative is tailored to ease public transport woes, lower commuting costs and modernise the fleet by reducing import [...] The post Bus imports now duty-free appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.description: Source: Council meets on filling top posts – herald Diana Nherera Herald Reporter THE City of Harare’s 1945th Ordinary council meeting yesterday discussed interviews for senior officials, for the planning and the human resources departments. In an interview, Harare Mayor Councillor Jacob Mafume said the full council meeting held at the townhouse also dealt with the [...] The post Council meets on filling top posts appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Woman stabs taxi driver
more.description: Source: Woman stabs taxi driver – herald Yeukai Karengezeka-Chisepo Court Correspondent A WOMAN from Borrowdale suburb in Harare has appeared in court after allegedly stabbing a hired driver multiple times in a shocking daylight attack. Michelle Nhukarume appeared before Harare magistrate Ms Ayanda Dhlamini facing an assault charge. She was granted bail of US$100. The [...] The post Woman stabs taxi driver appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Farmers upbeat as tobacco prices show improvement
more.description: Source: Farmers upbeat as tobacco prices show improvement – herald Precious Manomano Herald Reporter TOBACCO farmers have expressed cautious optimism over this year’s marketing season, saying current prices, while not exceptional, are good enough to sustain their livelihoods and encourage continued production. Across auction and contract floors, growers are steadily delivering their crop as harvesting [...] The post Farmers upbeat as tobacco prices show improvement appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Govt reaffirms commitment to Gwanda–Maphisa Road
more.description: Source: Govt reaffirms commitment to Gwanda–Maphisa Road – herald Sukulwenkosi Dube‐Matutu VICE President Kembo Mohadi has emphasised Government’s commitment to the completion of the Gwanda–Maphisa Road rehabilitation, which is expected to unlock economic development in Matabeleland South. Construction of the Gwanda–Maphisa Road, which had stalled for decades, resumed earlier this year, with six contractors currently [...] The post Govt reaffirms commitment to Gwanda–Maphisa Road appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Technical consolidation of Media Practitioners Bill underway
more.description: Source: Technical consolidation of Media Practitioners Bill underway – herald Walter Nyamukondiwa in KADOMA GOVERNMENT has started the technical consolidation of the draft Media Practitioners Bill, which is expected to entrench professionalism and ethical conduct in journalism practice. The Bill is expected to curb sensationalism, weaponisation of information, free-loading and the proliferation of unethical and [...] The post Technical consolidation of Media Practitioners Bill underway appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .
